summary: Story 1.7 is implementation-ready for trip/period record creation. It is source-backed, tenant-safe, scoped to the existing memory record route/editor/service flow, and gives @dev enough implementation and testing context without expanding into media delivery, filters, visibility controls, maps, masonry, account flows, or album-generation language.

template_compliance.notes: Required story sections are present and populated for pre-implementation: Status, Executor Assignment, Story, Acceptance Criteria, Integration Verification, CodeRabbit Integration, Tasks / Subtasks, Dev Notes, Testing, Change Log, Dev Agent Record, and QA Results. Future-owned Dev Agent Record and QA Results placeholders remain, which is expected before implementation.

executor_assignment.notes: Executor @dev and quality_gate @architect are known, distinct agents. quality_gate_tools is non-empty and aligned to the code/frontend/backend route, service, Livewire, timeline, and route-access slice.

dependency_sequence.notes: Dependencies on Stories 1.1, 1.2, 1.4a, 1.4b, 1.4c, 1.5, and 1.6 are explicit. The Story 1.6 file still contains a textual lifecycle mismatch, but the DevOps handoff reports PR #12 merged, release PASS, tag v1.7.0, and this branch prepared from merged origin/main.

file_structure.notes: The story identifies the correct Laravel memory product locations: routes/web.php, app/Models/MemoryRecord.php, app/Services/MemoryRecordService.php, app/Services/MemoryTimelineService.php, app/Livewire/Memory/MemoryRecordEditor.php, app/Livewire/Memory/MemoryRecordDetail.php, Blade views, translations, and focused PHPUnit test paths.

ui_frontend_completeness.notes: The UI guidance is specific enough for implementation: period flow leads with title, location, start date, end date, people, notes, and Memory Highlights while diary creation/editing remains led by body and experience date. Scope exclusions prevent media, filters, map, masonry, public sharing, and album-generation drift.

acceptance_criteria_coverage.notes: Tasks map to all five acceptance criteria and all three integration verification points, including date validation, period-start timeline placement, tenant privacy, structured form behavior, diary regression safety, and cross-tenant denial.

testing_guidance.notes: The story requires focused PHPUnit/Livewire coverage for period route access, structured rendering, successful creation, invalid dates, service validation, tags, people, ordered highlights, timeline placement, unsupported type denial, cross-tenant denial, and diary create/edit regression safety.

security_scope.notes: Tenant membership, completed-profile route gating, policy authorization before writes, route-tenant record scoping, generic cross-tenant denial, and avoidance of public/private media changes are explicit in AC/IV, scope boundaries, tasks, and CodeRabbit focus areas.

coderabbit_integration.notes: CodeRabbit is enabled in core-config.yaml and the story contains story type analysis, specialized agents, required quality gate tasks, self-healing behavior, and focus areas matching frontend/API/security risk.

code_intelligence.notes: Code intelligence duplicate detection was skipped because the local code-intel availability helper was not used in this session and the validate-next-story task permits silent skip when unavailable.

anti_hallucination.notes: Technical claims were cross-checked against PRD Story 1.7, architecture memory_records and timeline guidance, UI architecture route/test guidance, framework coding/source-tree rules, current routes/model/service/Livewire code, the SM-to-PO handoff, and the DevOps-to-SM Story 1.7 handoff.

dev_readiness.notes: The story is self-contained enough for @dev to implement without inventing requirements. The implementation path is constrained to existing service, Livewire, route, model, translation, detail, timeline, and test surfaces.

nice_to_have: Reconcile stale Story 1.6 textual lifecycle status separately; do not block Story 1.7 because the DevOps handoff establishes the merged baseline.
During implementation, keep period edit polish minimal and only where shared editor type-safety requires it; deeper edit UX remains outside this creation story.
